summary: postinstall hook exfiltrates cloud IAM credentials and host recon to oastify.com C2
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall script executes a chain of curl commands against multiple cloud instance-metadata services (AWS IMDS at 169.254.169.254 requesting IAM role credentials for 'sandyclaw-canary-worker-role', Alibaba metadata at 100.100.100.200, Tencent metadata at metadata.tencentyun.com / 169.254.0.23, and an internal SSRF endpoint), writes the responses to /tmp/aws.txt and similar files, then POSTs the concatenated metadata plus `ls -la /data/` output to http://h4mx6b7krgzarfehbutwabxbu20tojc8.oastify.com (a Burp Collaborator / DNS-logging endpoint) over plain HTTP. Any installer running this on a cloud VM, CI runner, or container with IMDS reachable will leak short-lived IAM credentials and host filesystem state to an attacker-controlled collector. The behavior fires automatically on default install with no user interaction.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
